non_blocking_risks.risk: If new evidence arrives after solution-comparison.json is stabilized, candidate ranking and fallback conditions may need to be recomputed together.

profile_adherence.evidence: The run stayed autonomous and deep, pulled bounded search forward when evidence gaps were real, and continued into explicit candidate comparison without routine confirmation chatter.

same_source_consistency.evidence: The bounded search evidence, selected generation targets, candidate pool, and evaluated paths all refer to the same source-aligned task object.

cross_format_consistency.evidence: The JSON generation artifacts and the XML evaluation report use the same candidate ids, target references, and fallback logic.

fact_validation_chain.item: Confirm search-notes.json records bounded evidence and unresolved questions rather than a full modeling artifact.
Confirm selected-targets.json uses that evidence to choose generation targets explicitly.
Confirm operator-calls.json binds operator families to the selected targets.
Confirm candidate-pool.json records expected_gain, new_cost, and evidence_needed for each candidate.
Confirm solution-comparison.json and solution-paths.json remain downstream of the candidate pool rather than replacing it.
Confirm fallback-rules.json exposes trigger_condition and fallback_target explicitly when a fallback path exists.

unverifiable_assumptions.item: The bounded first-search pass already captured the highest-priority evidence needed for the current comparison round.

check.note: search-notes.json, candidate generation artifacts, and implementation-shaping artifacts are all present and staged coherently.

scope_alignment_checks.item: The round keeps bounded retrieval, candidate generation, and implementation shaping inside the source-aligned core and does not widen into runtime operations.

check.note: search-notes.json was created as bounded writeback evidence before later candidate generation.
selected-targets.json, operator-calls.json, and candidate-pool.json were created before comparison and route selection.
solution-comparison.json, solution-paths.json, and validation-plan.json exist before finish logic.
implementation-steps.json and fallback-rules.json carry the evaluated path into executable next-step logic.

dispatch_recommendations.item: Proceed because retrieval writeback, candidate generation, evaluation, and fallback shaping are all explicit and staged in the correct order.

check.note: The autonomous deep profile justified one bounded retrieval-to-generation probe and kept it inside the source-aligned core.

check.why: Without it, target selection and evidence_needed fields would drift away from explicit evidence gaps.
check.evidence: selected-targets.json and candidate-pool.json both depend on bounded search writeback rather than unsupported inference.

check.why: It is the pivot artifact between bounded generation and later comparison, validation, and fallback routing.
check.evidence: solution-comparison.json, solution-paths.json, validation-plan.json, and fallback-rules.json all depend on it.

profile_adjustment_recommendation.adjustment_reason: The current profile fit the round because bounded retrieval and downstream evaluation both benefited from proactive expansion without user-interrupting chatter.

evaluation_report.score_reason: The example now covers a real source-layer interaction chain, but pluginization and self-upgrade still remain outside the standard example set.
